content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN non-negative integers.

It is guaranteed that AAA contains **at least two distinct** elements.

You are also given a non-negative integer XXX.

For each YYY (0ŌēżYŌēżX)(0 \leq Y \leq X)(0ŌēżYŌēżX), we create an array BBB of size NNN such that Bi=AiŌłŻYB_i = A_i | YBiŌĆŗ=AiŌĆŗŌłŻY, where ŌłŻ|ŌłŻ denotes the bitwise OR operation.

Find the **largest** integer Y(YŌēżX)Y(Y \leq X)Y(YŌēżX) such that the array BBB contains **at least two distinct** elements.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and XXX ŌĆö the length of the array AAA and maximum value of YYY allowed.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,ŌĆ”,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1ŌĆŗ,A2ŌĆŗ,ŌĆ”,ANŌĆŗ,  representing the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the largest YYY satisfying the condition.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2Ōŗģ1052 \cdot 10^52Ōŗģ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: For Y=5Y = 5Y=5, B=[1ŌłŻ5,1ŌłŻ5,2ŌłŻ5]=[5,5,7]B = [1|5, 1|5, 2|5] = [5, 5, 7]B=[1ŌłŻ5,1ŌłŻ5,2ŌłŻ5]=[5,5,7], which has 222 distinct elements.
Thus, this is the largest value of YYY.
Test case 222: For Y=10Y = 10Y=10, B=[4ŌłŻ10,5ŌłŻ10,2ŌłŻ10,3ŌłŻ10,5ŌłŻ10]=[14,15,10,11,15]B = [4|10, 5|10, 2|10, 3|10, 5|10] = [14, 15, 10, 11, 15]B=[4ŌłŻ10,5ŌłŻ10,2ŌłŻ10,3ŌłŻ10,5ŌłŻ10]=[14,15,10,11,15], which has 444 distinct elements.
Thus, this is the largest value of YYY.
Test case 333: For Y=1Y = 1Y=1, B=[1ŌłŻ1,1ŌłŻ1,3ŌłŻ1]=[1,1,3]B = [1|1, 1|1, 3|1] = [1, 1, 3]B=[1ŌłŻ1,1ŌłŻ1,3ŌłŻ1]=[1,1,3], which has 222 distinct elements.
For Y=2Y = 2Y=2, B=[1ŌłŻ2,1ŌłŻ2,3ŌłŻ2]=[3,3,3]B = [1|2, 1|2, 3|2] = [3, 3, 3]B=[1ŌłŻ2,1ŌłŻ2,3ŌłŻ2]=[3,3,3], which has 111 distinct element only.
